source_quote: In population with baseline PU area <=8 cm2, mean absolute decrease in wound area at week 6 was significantly greater in the OKG group compared to the placebo (-2.3 +/- 4.2 cm2 vs. -1.7 +/- 1.7 cm2; p = 0.006)
The mean wound area reduction for baseline area <= 8 cm2 are -59.5 +/- 71.4% and -54.0 +/- 69.0% for the OKG group and placebo groups respectively (p=0.477, Mann-Whitney test)
33AEs reported in 22 patients (15 OKG, 7 placebo) were considered related to study medication. All this AEs were mild to moderate.

outcome: All-cause mortality up to 12 months' follow-up
source_quote: In the control group, two patients had sudden cardiac death during the study period.

outcome: Hospital admissions (all-cause) up to 12 months' follow-up
source_quote: three patients were excluded due to worsening of heart failure symptoms, one of them developed orthopnea and progressed to cardiogenic pulmonary edema, so he was admitted to ICU to receive mechanical ventilation. The other two patients had bilateral lower limb cardiac edema, night cough, exertional dyspnea with tachycardia (decompensated heart failure) and they were admitted to the hospital.
